prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Arrr, gather 'round ye scurvy dogs and listen close, fer I, Captain Blackbeard meself—or close enough in spirit—be here to spin ye the tale o' why the heavens above be painted blue as a siren's silken sails!

'Tis nae sorcery nor the devil's own brush, nay, but a trick o' the sun's fierce rays dancin' through the salty air what we pirates know so well. See, the light from that golden orb up there be full o' colors, like the rainbow after a storm at sea. But when it plunges into our world's misty veil o' vapors and dust, the shorter waves— them blue ones, sly as a cutlass in the dark—get scattered every which way by the tiniest specks floatin' about. The longer reds and yellows plow straight through like a broadside cannon, leavin' the blue to bounce and frolic across the sky, fillin' yer eyes with its cheeky glow!

On a clear day, 'tis why the vault above looks like a vast ocean o' azure, mockin' us salty souls below. But come sunset, when the sun dips low like a rum-soaked horizon, the blue scatters off, and ye get them fiery oranges and reds—arrr, a pirate's perfect farewell to the day! So hoist yer colors high and ponder that, ye landlubbers, fer the sky's blue be the sea's own kin, scattered by the winds o' fate! Yo ho ho!
